meta.setting: County record room in a northern California mining town
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in records, testimony, and social pressure

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Courthouse, old records, iron shutters, brick ruins, and mining-town civic texture.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Displacement and official-record pressure as context for contested memory.

image_prompt.subject: three courthouse memory women in an 1860s-style county room, sorting deeds, mourning ribbons, testimony notes, and a locked drawer while men argue outside the door
image_prompt.scene: warm lamplight, iron shutter shadows, cracked plaster, paper stacks, shawls, a stove ticking low, and unreadable docket pages spread across a scarred table
image_prompt.composition: square interior with the women centered but busy with different tasks; doorway tension behind them, records and ribbons in the foreground
image_prompt.camera: calm eye-level tableau with game-cinematic lighting, still enough for the viewer to read posture and objects
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make intelligence visible in posture and arrangement, not in dramatic pointing. The room should feel like it can outlast any man shouting outside it.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine civic interior
render_traits: slightly simplified human silhouettes with grounded facial detail
modest texture resolution on paper, wool, iron, wood, and plaster
hard ambient occlusion under table edges, papers, drawers, and stove
warm lamp light with hard shutter shadows
quiet social-pressure composition rather than courtroom spectacle
style_modifiers.palette: lamp amber, paper cream, ink blue-black, wool brown, iron gray, mourning black, brick red shadow, and stove soot.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable docket entries, labels, letters, names, or legal text. Papers must be visibly written but illegible.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The women, record table, papers, ribbons, doorway, and room texture are fully readable.
Every visible prop and garment belongs plausibly to an 1899 county room.
The image shows memory, testimony, and social pressure without theatrical melodrama.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
